title: Repeating groups use once() to anchor the repeat key
description: Forms with repeating groups (AE, CM, MH, DV, PR, and any custom log-line form) must include a calculate field at the top of the repeating group whose calculation uses once(...@ItemGroupRepeatKey) to prevent the repeat key from being overwritten on edit. Pair with a separate display-only text field using if(${ID}!='', ${ID}, 'Scheduled') to give the data-entry user a visible per-row label.
rationale: OC4 reassigns @ItemGroupRepeatKey on every save unless once() locks the value. Without the anchor, cross-row references and audit trails break when a user edits an existing repeat instance. The paired display field makes the repeat instance identifiable in the data-entry UI without exposing the internal key.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} has a repeating group; verify the first row inside the repeat block is a calculate field anchored with once(...@ItemGroupRepeatKey), paired with a display-only text field using if(${ID}!='', ${ID}, 'Scheduled').

effect.soft: Emit two rows at the top of the repeat block: (1) a calculate row with calculation using once(...@ItemGroupRepeatKey); (2) a display-only text row with calculation if(${ID}!='', ${ID}, 'Scheduled') and readonly=yes. The first locks the repeat key; the second gives data entry a visible per-instance label.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-5 during Phase B.1a. target=form: the trigger is form-level (has_repeating_group), the anchor is form-level (top of repeat block), the row-pair emission is delegated to the soft directive. Mechanically verifying that the first survey row inside a repeat block matches the once()-anchor pattern requires DSL extension; deferred to Phase B/C. OC-5a (element-type column matrix) and OC-5b (visible-calculated uses text type) live in separate convention files as peer rules. Kind revised from `structured` to `hybrid` during B.1a translation because the two-row pattern at the top of the repeat block (calculate with once(), display-only text with if()) isn't expressible in the current DSL; soft directive carries the pattern guidance.
